title: vernonia greggii subsp. schaffneri
intro: Vernonia greggii subsp. schaffneri, also known as Schaffner's ironweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the sunflower family and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Schaffner's ironweed have been used to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, pain relief, and skin irritations, and may also exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve for its herbal benefits, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
